metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (via the correct prerequisite ticket) that `AnalyzerResult.loc` is a structured sub-object exposing `.max_loc`, and add that type/shape to this plan's context; if `.loc` is a scalar, update the AC access path accordingly.
evidence: ABSENCE: No definition of AnalyzerResult.loc's type found in repo via search_files
Ticket 1a05-e3a8-207f-462a (cited prerequisite) does not define .loc as a structured object
Cached reviews (e3bd run) note: 'the prerequisite ticket (3b30) defines .loc only as a plain optional field without specifying it has a .max_loc sub-attribute'
The only `max_loc` found in the codebase is a dict key in git_metrics.py (unrelated to AnalyzerResult.loc)
Plan body says 'reads its .loc field' (the whole field), while the AC says 'returns its .loc.max_loc' — the body/AC are inconsistent on what .loc is
findings.finding: The AC asserts `result.loc.max_loc` as the return value, treating `.loc` as a structured sub-object with a `.max_loc` attribute, but no evidence in the repo or in the (incorrectly) cited prerequisite ticket confirms that `AnalyzerResult.loc` is anything other than a plain scalar/optional field. Prior plan-review cache entries consistently identify this as an unverified nested-access assumption.
scenarios: If .loc is a plain integer or optional scalar, calling .loc.max_loc raises AttributeError at runtime
An implementer following the plan body ('reads its .loc field') would return the whole .loc object, not .loc.max_loc, causing a spec mismatch with the AC test

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the AC access path with the actual shape of `AnalyzerResult.loc` as defined in 3b30: either clarify that `.loc` is a structured object with a `.max_loc` sub-field (and update 3b30 accordingly), or correct the AC to `result.loc` if `.loc` is the scalar value being read.
evidence: Ticket 3b30-2168-ba16-41fc AC: 'Analyzer protocol + AnalyzerResult (fields loc/complexity/duplication, each optional=None when unsupported)' — no sub-fields named
Multiple cached plan-review findings (e.g. e3bd-8cd2-35f1-406b) independently flagged '.loc.max_loc' as inconsistent with 3b30's plain-field definition
src/rebar/metrics/git_metrics.py uses 'max_loc' as a dict key in a separate git-metrics context, not as a sub-attribute of AnalyzerResult.loc
findings.finding: The AC access path `result.loc.max_loc` treats `AnalyzerResult.loc` as a nested object with a `.max_loc` sub-attribute, but ticket 3b30 (the authoritative definer) specifies `AnalyzerResult` fields as `loc/complexity/duplication, each optional=None when unsupported` — a plain optional scalar field, not a structured object. No `.max_loc` sub-field is defined anywhere in 3b30's plan or file_impact.
scenarios: If .loc is an int/float/None scalar, accessing .loc.max_loc raises AttributeError at runtime and the test fails unconditionally.
If .loc is intended to be a structured object with .max_loc, that shape must be specified in 3b30 or a separate ticket — neither currently does so.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit file targets to the plan: name the source file for the reader function (e.g. `src/rebar/metrics/eval_reader.py`) and confirm `tests/unit/metrics/test_eval_reader.py` as a new file this ticket creates.
evidence: list_directory(tests/unit/metrics) shows no test_eval_reader.py — only test_event_metrics.py, test_git_metrics.py, test_registry.py, test_sidecar_metrics.py, test_snapshot.py, test_transcript_adapter.py, etc.
Plan body and AC name no source file (e.g. src/rebar/metrics/eval_reader.py) where the reader function will be created
No prerequisite ticket in the dependency chain (1a05 or 3b30) has file_impact targeting test_eval_reader.py or any eval_reader module
findings.finding: The test file `tests/unit/metrics/test_eval_reader.py` referenced in the AC proving command does not exist in the repository, and no prerequisite or upstream ticket creates it. The plan names no file/module where the reader function itself will live, leaving both the implementation file and the test file as unspecified edit targets.
scenarios: An implementer has no specified location to create the reader function or the test, making the edit target ambiguous.
The proving command will fail with 'file not found' until both the source and test files are created — but neither is named in the plan's scope.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Files touched' section listing `src/rebar/metrics/eval_reader.py` (new: reader function) and `tests/unit/metrics/test_eval_reader.py` (new: proves test_reads_loc).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the `.loc.max_loc` access path in the AC with the `AnalyzerResult.loc` type contract defined in prerequisite 3b30: either (a) specify in 3b30 that `.loc` is a structured object with a `.max_loc` sub-field, or (b) correct the AC to `result.loc` if `.loc` is the scalar LOC value, or (c) name an intermediate type (e.g. `LocResult`) and confirm 3b30 will define it.
evidence: Plan AC: 'a reader function accepts an `AnalyzerResult` and returns its `.loc.max_loc`'
Plan body: 'This ticket only reads its `.loc` field' — conflicts with `.loc.max_loc` nested access
Prerequisite ticket 3b30 AC: '`AnalyzerResult` (fields loc/complexity/duplication, each optional=None when unsupported)' — no sub-field structure specified for `.loc`
Cached reviewer findings in .rebar/cache/plan-review/e3bd-8cd2-35f1-406b/ independently surface this same structural mismatch across multiple samples
findings.finding: The AC specifies `result.loc.max_loc`, treating `AnalyzerResult.loc` as a structured sub-object with a `.max_loc` attribute, but the prerequisite ticket (3b30) defines `.loc` only as a plain optional field — its type (int vs. nested object) is unspecified. If `.loc` is a scalar (the natural reading of a 'loc' field), the access path `result.loc.max_loc` will raise AttributeError at runtime, and the test will fail to prove what the plan intends. The plan body also contradicts the AC by saying 'reads its `.loc` field' (implying the whole field is the value), while the AC returns `.loc.max_loc` (implying `.loc` is a structured sub-object).
scenarios: Implementer follows the prerequisite (3b30) and defines `AnalyzerResult.loc` as `Optional[int]` — the reader calling `result.loc.max_loc` raises `AttributeError`, the AC test fails.
Implementer assumes `.loc` must be a nested object and invents a `LocResult(max_loc=...)` type not specified anywhere, creating a spec drift between 3b30 and this ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: If 'loc' in the code-health context means a distribution/summary (like `git_metrics`'s `max_loc`, `count`, etc.), then the plan should explicitly state that `AnalyzerResult.loc` is a structured object (e.g. a dataclass with `max_loc` and other sub-fields) and update the 3b30 prerequisite AC accordingly. If `.loc` is simply a line-count integer, correct the AC to `return result.loc` and rename the test assertion to match.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit `depends_on: [rebar:1a05-e3a8-207f-462a]` dependency edge to this ticket AND confirm that the prerequisite ticket's plan/file_impact affirmatively establishes `AnalyzerResult.loc.max_loc` (not just `AnalyzerResult` in general) so the proving command is unblocked and item 9 is cleared.
evidence: Plan body: 'This leaf consumes the `AnalyzerResult` protocol `AnalyzerResult [rebar:1a05-e3a8-207f-462a]`'
Plan body: 'This ticket only reads its `.loc` field'
The proving command will fail at import/runtime if the prerequisite has not landed or does not expose `.loc.max_loc`
FAIL-CLOSED rule: cannot confirm dependency edge or that C's plan establishes `.loc.max_loc` specifically
findings.finding: The proving command `pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_eval_reader.py::test_reads_loc` will import and exercise `AnalyzerResult` (specifically its `.loc.max_loc` attribute) from the prerequisite ticket `[rebar:1a05-e3a8-207f-462a]`, but the plan does not confirm that a dependency edge exists between this ticket and that prerequisite, nor that the prerequisite's plan affirmatively establishes `.loc.max_loc`. Per FAIL-CLOSED, taxonomy item 9 stands: the test references a sibling ticket's symbols without a verified dependency edge and confirmed coverage.
scenarios: Prerequisite ticket is incomplete or exposes a different attribute name (e.g. `.loc.maximum` instead of `.loc.max_loc`), causing the test to error rather than fail cleanly.
Ticket is scheduled before the prerequisite lands, making the proving command structurally unable to pass.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `depends_on: [rebar:1a05-e3a8-207f-462a]` in the ticket metadata and add a plan note such as: 'Prerequisite [rebar:1a05-e3a8-207f-462a] delivers `AnalyzerResult` with a `.loc` field of type `LocMetrics` exposing `.max_loc: int`; this ticket may not be scheduled until that ticket is merged.'

evidence: citation [rebar:1a05-e3a8-207f-462a] ('This leaf consumes the `AnalyzerResult` protocol `AnalyzerResult') names no VERIFIED upstream prerequisite: P neither declares `depends_on -> 1a05-e3a8-207f-462a` nor is blocked by it. Add a `depends_on` edge to 1a05-e3a8-207f-462a, ensure 1a05-e3a8-207f-462a `blocks` this ticket, or remove the citation. Until then the cited symbol is grounded as normal (fails closed).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the plan body to specify that the reader returns `.loc.max_loc` (not the whole `.loc` field), aligning it with the AC.
evidence: Body: 'This ticket only reads its `.loc` field.'
AC: 'a reader function accepts an `AnalyzerResult` and returns its `.loc.max_loc`'
findings.finding: The plan body describes reading `.loc` (the whole field) while the AC specifies returning `.loc.max_loc` (a specific sub-field). An implementer reading only the body might return the entire `.loc` object rather than the `.max_loc` sub-field.
scenarios: An implementer writes a function returning the full `.loc` object, satisfying the body description but failing the test which asserts `.loc.max_loc`.
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'This ticket only reads its `.loc` field' to 'This ticket reads and returns the `.loc.max_loc` sub-field of an `AnalyzerResult`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the title/body mismatch: either (a) add an explicit step that surfaces and documents the uncovered prerequisite (e.g., a BLOCKED/DEPENDS-ON note referencing the gap in `AnalyzerResult [rebar:1a05-e3a8-207f-462a]`), or (b) retitle the plan to reflect that it purely adds a reader function.
evidence: Title: 'consumes AnalyzerResult citing an uncovered prerequisite'
Body: 'This ticket only reads its `.loc` field.'
AC: 'a reader function accepts an `AnalyzerResult` and returns its `.loc.max_loc`' — no prerequisite documentation step
findings.finding: The plan title promises a prerequisite-discovery/flagging task ('citing an uncovered prerequisite') but the plan body and AC implement only a reader function that returns `.loc.max_loc` — no prerequisite gap is surfaced, documented, or resolved anywhere in the plan.
findings.location: Plan title vs. Plan body / Acceptance criteria
scenarios: An implementer following the body builds only a reader and ships it; the 'uncovered prerequisite' concern named in the title is never addressed, leaving a latent dependency risk undocumented.
A reviewer expecting prerequisite analysis finds none and cannot determine whether the dependency gap was intentionally deferred or simply forgotten.
findings.suggested_fix: If the intent is only to add the reader, retitle to 'Add AnalyzerResult loc reader for code-health rendering'. If the intent includes flagging the prerequisite, add an explicit AC such as: 'Document that `AnalyzerResult [rebar:1a05-e3a8-207f-462a]` is an uncovered prerequisite in the project tracker before merging.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence motivation: state why the current codebase lacks this reader (e.g., 'No existing function extracts `.loc.max_loc` from `AnalyzerResult`; callers currently inline this access or skip it entirely.'), or cite the failing/missing test that demonstrates the gap.
evidence: Body only states: 'This leaf consumes the `AnalyzerResult` protocol … to render a code-health value.'
No reproduction steps, no Expected/Actual, no named defect or gap in current behavior.
ABSENCE: no explanation of why the existing codebase cannot already return `.loc.max_loc`.
findings.finding: The plan adds a new mechanism (reader function) but provides no motivation for why this reader does not already exist or what current system gap it fills — necessity is asserted implicitly ('render a code-health value') without any reproduction, expected-vs-actual, or named defect.
scenarios: The reader already exists under a different name; adding a duplicate creates confusion.
The AnalyzerResult protocol does not yet expose `.loc`; the reader is built against an interface that doesn't exist, and the plan has no prerequisite check.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the cited prerequisite `[rebar:1a05-e3a8-207f-462a]` (a T8 bug report) with the ticket that actually defines the `AnalyzerResult` protocol (e.g. `3b30-2168-ba16-41fc` per cached plan-review evidence), and verify that ticket's plan/file_impact affirmatively establishes `.loc` as a structured sub-object with a `.max_loc` attribute.
evidence: Ticket 1a05-e3a8-207f-462a title: 'Plan-review T8 overlay over-fires on non-LLM tickets (dataclass/enum vocabulary misread as an LLM output-schema)' — no AnalyzerResult definition present
Ticket 1a05-e3a8-207f-462a description mentions AnalyzerResult only as an example data shape in a bug narrative, not as a protocol being defined or established
Ticket 1a05-e3a8-207f-462a has no file_impact entries and no verify_commands relating to AnalyzerResult
ABSENCE: `search_files('class AnalyzerResult')` returns no matches in the repo
Cache entries for prior reviews of this plan consistently flag the mismatch between the cited ticket and the actual AnalyzerResult contract
findings.finding: The plan cites `[rebar:1a05-e3a8-207f-462a]` as the source of the `AnalyzerResult` protocol, but that ticket is a bug report about T8 plan-review over-firing — it defines no `AnalyzerResult` class, no `.loc` field, and no `.loc.max_loc` contract. The CITED-PREREQUISITE ESCAPE requires affirmative confirmation that the cited ticket establishes the specific relied-upon functionality; the wrong ticket citation leaves the core prerequisite unbacked.
scenarios: Implementer looks up the cited prerequisite to understand the AnalyzerResult contract and finds a bug report — no .loc field spec, no .loc.max_loc shape, no protocol to code against

The actual AnalyzerResult-defining ticket (likely 3b30-2168-ba16-41fc per cached review evidence) is not declared as a dependency, so its edge is never verified and the work may proceed without that ticket being complete
findings.suggested_fix: Update the plan body to cite the correct prerequisite ticket that defines AnalyzerResult (the ticket that specifies AnalyzerResult with fields loc/complexity/duplication). Then confirm that ticket's spec explicitly names .loc.max_loc (or the sub-object type of .loc) so the access path in the AC is grounded.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `[rebar:1a05-e3a8-207f-462a]` with the correct prerequisite citation `[rebar:3b30-2168-ba16-41fc]` (the ticket that actually defines `AnalyzerResult` in `src/rebar/metrics/analyzer.py`) and add a `depends_on` edge to 3b30.
evidence: Ticket 1a05-e3a8-207f-462a title: 'Plan-review T8 overlay over-fires on non-LLM tickets' — no AnalyzerResult definition, no file_impact touching analyzer.py
Ticket 3b30-2168-ba16-41fc defines: 'Analyzer protocol + AnalyzerResult (fields loc/complexity/duplication)' and file_impact: src/rebar/metrics/analyzer.py (new)
Neither src/rebar/metrics/analyzer.py nor any AnalyzerResult symbol exists in the current repo tree (list_directory confirms)
findings.finding: The plan cites `AnalyzerResult [rebar:1a05-e3a8-207f-462a]`, but ticket 1a05-e3a8-207f-462a is the T8 plan-review over-firing bug and does not define `AnalyzerResult` at all. The actual definer is ticket 3b30-2168-ba16-41fc (`src/rebar/metrics/analyzer.py`). Because the cited ticket does not establish the relied-upon symbol, the citation cannot be credited and `AnalyzerResult` is an ungrounded dependency.
scenarios: An implementer following the citation is led to the wrong ticket; the actual AnalyzerResult shape/location is unknowable from the cited ticket's content.

The Layer-1 edge check (as implemented by 266e-c8b6-0cb0-44ae) also rejects this citation: 266e's own deps show 'relates_to' not 'depends_on' for 1a05, and 1a05 does not carry a 'blocks' edge back to this ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the plan body to: 'AnalyzerResult protocol `AnalyzerResult [rebar:3b30-2168-ba16-41fc]`' and add `depends_on: 3b30-2168-ba16-41fc` to this ticket's dependency edges.
